Those consequences are no simple political game. You might fail to even bear them. That, I can determine for you.
One quick question. If it were you?
Then the great winter swim would already be in full swing.
Hah.
Let's trust in Degenbrecher.
Trust she won't fall.

lines.st: At some unknown point, the first wound appears on her body.
It lies about her shoulder, a slash over her suit, an imperceptible trace of blood drawn.
Compared to the kinds of wounds she's used to, this one's worth no mention.
Degenbrecher fells the few soldiers about her. Ironically, the subtle sting only sharpens the heady mix of fear her eyes strike into you, makes her movements that much more steely cold.
The second wound is brought on by Originium Arts.
An average caster means nothing to Degenbrecher, but the numbers advantage mutates the nature of the Originium Arts' efficacy.
The casters among the troops cast behind their human wall. Fire, ice and thunder burst forth in unison, with staggering momentum.

Degenbrecher flings her weapons, running one of the casters' shoulders clean through, while she stubbornly approaches through the full length of their Arts' range, and plows straight into the soldiers' midst. That puts an end to that display.
The third wound comes as the artillerymen aim a third ballista volley at her.
An opponent armed to the teeth is no fear of Degenbrecher's, but with limbs less than free, and ballistae pestering her in spades, it does get a little annoying to cope.
A shell slips through, and lands a scorch mark on her abdomen.
Her skin is seared in an instant, but hardly any blood comes. That's good, all things considered.
Degenbrecher is under no impression she'll lose.
She's just not too sure she has enough blood to last much longer.
The fourth wound, the fifth...
She loses track.
Counting them is a pain.

lines.x: It's not enough...
Again!
Urgh!
Lisburn!
Charles! Withdraw Lisburn to the rear!
You staunch his bleeding, I'll be right there with first aid—!
*cough*...
She's giving out!
Now's our chance! Charge!
Hah.
As long as I can still move a finger...
Khakkhhh!
I can handle any of you.
Madam Degenbrecher!
Surely you're reaching your limits too?!
If you really meant to fight us out-and-out, you wouldn't have drawn out this situation in the first place!
Fight like this any longer, and I fear death will come for somebody...
Isn't that exactly the kind of war you Victorians make an art of starting?
The only difference here is, your opponent is me alone.
Harold, you're a soldier.
And I—am your enemy.
...Ha-hah, and keenly put, Madam Degenbrecher.
But I don't believe for a second that you're blind to it.
Karlan Trade's private operations were ferreted out by the Trilby Ashers, and I don't think the Duke will rest on her laurels about it!
That—*cough*—is Enciodes's problem.
This is the very last chance they're leaving Master Enciodes!
He need only bend an ear to the Duke, he need only part with a sliver of the cake...
Be it just 30% of what we proposed! I could still take that back to the Duke and try, for heaven's sakes!
This was never even a war you needed to win...
How stupid.
Who do you say that as?
You're window dressing. You're a tourist. You're a burdenbeast vet.
Have you been at leisure for so long, you've begun to believe your own lies?
—No.
You've never forgotten who you are.
You hate war, absolutely.
You abandoned what real power was in your hands. You took what men of yours sympathized. You left the maelstrom far behind, and fled to Kjerag.
To a land where you thought war could never really happen.
But you were still a Victorian inside.
No.
It wasn't even something you had to remind yourself.
You might say that.
I am a Victorian soldier, Madam.
That much is ordained, and I might loathe it, and I might flee it.
But I could never sully Victoria's interests.
Good.
I'm the same.
Is she the same as this Victorian?
They don't seem very identical.
She's Leithanien-born, Kazimierz-raised, and only then came to Kjerag.
They don't need to know her name. All they know is she's born to fight solo, a victor of the knight competitions, the only one to ever win three years running.
Turn the clock further back, and she's a freak cut off from Originium Arts, a pariah in Leithanien.
Gaining and losing any of these things is dead simple.
They bore her to death.
But now, what she has isn't so easily lost, and she has no plans to lose them.
Now, they are the same.
This dull chat—*cough*—ends here.
I feel all too sorry for you, Madam Degenbrecher.
Oh, would that we hadn't come to this point. But if you wish it, then I choose to respect it.
I must stress at this closing hour that I am, through and through, your fan. The show you made in besting each opponent dazzled like nothing else.
This and this alone I ask you to believe. It cannot be lied.
Hah.
Noted.
It's my honor.
Riddled with scars, she raises her degenbrechers once more.
Just as she did in her first appearance before them all, Degenbrecher's point stabs into the ice.
The mist, constant over Lake Silberneherze, parts. A subdued, gargantuan rumble sinks in her enemies' stomachs.
Kchk. Kchk.
There comes the fine sound of cracking, and it's deafening.
Time to end this.
—! Order to all units!
Stop her breaking the ice! Regroup ASAP! Put down 'The Black Knight of Kazimierz'!
Nothing.
It's not in here.
Oh, and it's not here either.
Well, that just leaves...
Excuse me, sir, the train is speeding up! If you must wander, then please watch your step!
Oh, right, you haven't received your complimentary gift yet, have you? Can I ask what you'd like?
As compensation for your journey's delay, your purchase is completely free this time!
No need.
But, sir, it's a free gift...
Then I give this gift to you, madam.
Er, but, that's not...
Take it.
Now excuse me, if I could pass through?
A—Alright!
Thank you.
*huff*... *pant*...
Urp... oh, what in the land?
My legs are a little wobbly...
(Now, only the last cabin remains.)
(After all this time running around, I'm ragged.)
(I could retire... *sigh* Not when Londinium's still refinding its footing. Her Grace would never let me.)
(What will I find behind this door?)
(Their Kjerag Defense, hidden until this very moment, buried deeper than even their covert transport routes...)
(Whatever it is, it's a shame.)
It's a shame—Silverash will never get to meet his...
Huh?
What's up, panisko?
Sorry, we've got no seats left in our cabin. I think you want to try someplace else.
Uh, actually, I can just lea...
Did you need something, panisko?
I'm not kidding, we're full in here. I mean, you can see. We're bumping shoulders just trying to look around.
Yeah, that's why I'm saying I can absolutely just leave and find my own seat! I'm not kidding either!
You—You guys seriously don't gotta sit in a cab with me!
Don't be like that, Mr. Spokesman. You and us, we're invited together, aren't we? It was all one letter.
And this cabin, perfect amount of room for us all to sit. They look after you on the way too.
And with us around, the safety problem is no problem.
Safety's not my problem here, yeah, no...
I don't think it's 'perfect', is the thing?? You guys get to sit on the benches and I have to dump my butt on the table!
...Apologies, excuse me.
I see... I understand. I must be dreaming.
Kazimierz campaign knights and a KGCC representative spokesman sat in the same private cabin of a railway car...
So this is the defense Silverash plays.
How bold. How well and truly typical of the clan head's style.
Your Grace, I can't say it was for lack of trying...
But while duty might call me to fight a gaggle of campaign knights at once, I... think it's a little out of the question.
More than a little.
I guess your job's not easy either.
Stay calm.
Just passing through.
That uniform...
...This is quite the surprise party.
What is a Rhodes Islander doing here?
You'll have to ask my boss.

opts: Et tu, Caster's lackey?
Any questions, Mr. Bellingham?

lines.x: Quite the shocker indeed. I wasn't expecting you in the flesh.
But the funny thing is, weren't Rhodes Island and Kjerag only meant to be 'business partners'?
Yes, if there's one thing I can trust, it's my memory.
Many happy returns... 'Doctor'.
And we have a Rhodes Island Operator here? It seems the dossiers were right—we still haven't full stock of your company's many faces.
Still, that aside for now. No surprise could be more pleasant, meeting you here.
In all sincerity, I come with open arms this time. After all, I'm not even out of this disguise, and you recognize me by it.
Rhodes Island, you—
Yeesh, Doctor! A little help here!

opts: Muelsyse!

lines.x: Hmm? 'Sup?
Our stop's coming up! We gotta get the tech packed, Doc...
Oop? Who're you guys...?
Doctor, maybe we oughta bug the conductor to go a little faster...
Hey, it's you!
Rhodes Island and Columbia's Rhine Lab... well, well, well.
I see it's no coincidence you're all here, then.
Oh, jeez, I guess someone knows me already.
Doc, Mr. Sharp, Letobomb, aren't you gonna introduce this hatsy guy to me here?
Kjarr, I'd like to know. Would Kjeragandr bless the woman waging such a bloody battle for this statue?
Though she is born from other lands? Though in Kjeragandr, she has oh so little faith?
Heheh. That's a very easy question, Saintess.
The term 'Kjerag' simply means one who lives there, who adores this land, who finds a home in this nation.
It's no matter whether Kjeragandr recognizes it.
If a person identifies with it from the bottom of their heart, well, who can deny that?
Yes.
No one can deny that. Not even she, herself.
She is a Kjerag. She already was.
Kjarr, come with me to meet our visitors.
Our Victorian guests have come to partake in the ceremony. As Saintess, I have to make an adequate response.
At the very least, I can't go without saying hello.
I'd like to stress—the danger of this.
But... very well, Saintess.
Matriarch!
Sciurus is back!
She is?!
Some nerve being late... wait, no, I told her to keep out of this! Why did she come HERE?!
Who's saying I can't come?
And I didn't just come, I brought some special guests with me too!
Is this let's-bring-guests timing to you?
You make a language out of inane, you silly sister!
...I am NOT being silly.
Indeed. Madam Sciurus's hand is deft and wise. I find her admirable.
Ratatos, you'd do well to finally raise your opinion of her.
...What the hell is this, Enciodes?
Madam Sciurus, the guests have arrived safely.
Good! Bring them all in!
I'm out.
Master!
Your guests are here already!
Your who? These are MY guests I invited!
You're Madam Sciurus of the Browntail clan?
Very pleased you invited us to be part of this ceremony.
Sciurus... is this your pleasant surprise?
Uh, duh!
Let me introduce you all! We have here our campaign knight from the Adeptus Sprawiedliwi Kazimierz, here to celebrate the erection of our statue to Kjeragandr!
I sent an invitation especially to the Adeptus! That was NOT an easy invitation to make!
So they're MY guests!
It's just a coincidence, Madam Sciurus.
I cannot laud you enough that you were able to invite a campaign knight. My guest of honor, meanwhile, would be this fellow—
Welcome, Sir Spokesman of the General Chamber of Commerce.
Hello to you. I'm taking it you're Mr. Silverash?
Good to meetcha, good to meetcha, and hello from my company. Thank you for all the effort of inviting me out to Kjerag.
The scenery here in Kjerag, supremely beautiful. I believe your company and mine will absolutely end the day here with a partnership we both love.
Oh, and don't mind me but I gotta say, I think I saw some mad fight out on a frozen lake the whole way here?
And kind of familiar too, like, one guy. I mean, those combat moves, they rang bells in my head.
Oh sorry, excuse me if I'm talking too much?
It's, uh, a holdover from the old job. Ahem. Right, I'll be careful not to say too much that doesn't work towards us working together.
No, Mr. Mob, I'm in need of your exact talent right now.
Come again? You need...?
Yes.
Right now, this very moment.
Please, come with me. I'll have them cast sound amplification Arts.
May I ask you to loudly proclaim that—
Karlan Trade, and the corporations of the Kazimierz General Chamber of Commerce, are about to sign an agreement.
To Kazimierz, Kjerag will become a trusty partner.
...What's that noise?
It's not anyone splashing into the icewater, and it's not cries for help.
It sounds like someone loudly saying something, and then the people all about her stumble in disarray.
That loud voice. But whatever it's saying, she can't tell clearly.
Her field of view narrows and dims by a fraction more. The sounds almost turn to fuzz in her ears.
She can't feel her own limbs anymore, but she can be sure she still has a grip on her old companions.
Her degenbrechers.
Because she knows her body is still acting on reflex, striking down every person that comes near.
It's just... too loud.
Too loud. How can someone talk this noisily?
Why has the crowd gone?
Did someone breach her line of defense already?
No.
The wild beast's dulling eyes of gold suddenly flare alight.
—As long as she can stand.
—She will allow absolutely no one past!
...brecher! Madam Degenbrecher!
Stop fighting... Gah!
You've lost too much blood, Madam Degenbrecher! Come to your senses a little!
Aht-aht-aht, keep flailing at this rate and you'll sink us both in the lake!
We're not fighting?
No more fighting, no more!
Goodness, however much the Duke wants to slap Kjerag in the face, we can't be showing Kazimierz the hand in the process... at least not now.
Kept between Victoria and Kjerag, one might still call it the Duke's personal Silverash grievances. But the moment you bring Kazimierz into it too, things become far, far less simple.
Are the gourmands of Kazimierz already inviting Karlan Trade to the table? With such distance between them, and in such short order?
What a talented young individual.
And I know the Duke won't reproach me for deciding to stay my hand. Spiffing excuse.
A good half my men you batted into the lake, Madam Degenbrecher. It was a backbreaker to scoop them out again...
If we'd kept going like that, I'd need another pair of arms to treat you all!
I say you need them as is. *cough*
I take no criticism from anyone still hacking up blood.
...We're done?
Well and truly done.
Okay.
Enciodes pulled it off.
She did what she needed to.
That's all Degenbrecher hears.
Now, Madam, stop there!
The lives we live, they always tend to be out of our hands.
I wanted to retire eons ago myself, but I have to keep my own men, my all too often down-and-out brothers in mind.
With this, it finally ought to be my turn for once, hm?
And in light of the fact your faithful fan's been beaten black and blue...
Could you give me your autograph?
...You're still in the mood for that?
This card... heh.
Do tell?
The first time I won the Major, they wouldn't leave me alone until I agreed to a photoshoot.
After that, I never agreed to another one. They were too boring for me.
I see. No wonder The Black Knight's knight trumps always come in those same poses, edition after edition.
Degenbrecher accepts the pen Harold holds out to her, and writes her name—'Degenbrecher'.
Take it.
You'd better not have anything else for me to sign.
Ah, Madam, where are you going?
Do you need some quick treatment? My first aid's excellent, you know.
Degenbrecher lifts her head to look to the dawn.
In the distance, two figures walk, approaching her. She still can't make them out, but she knows who they are.
It's like Kjeragandr on high is gazing down on her, someplace as high as the sun.
No need.
I'm going back.
There's people waiting for me.
Ugh...

Uh... I...
Is it over? Did we win?
Ouch!
Good, at least you can still feel your arm.
Now get up if you're still alive.
Ouch! Take it easy, Charles!
Enough fooling around. Harold has enough work to do without you adding to it.
Get to the lakeside if you can still walk. Patch yourselves up if you can.
I need a few volunteers to help me carry the ones that can't get up themselves.
I'll come with you, Jefferson.
Just say it if you don't want that leg of yours anymore. We can have Harold make one for you.
God's wounds, I can't believe everyone's still breathing after all that. What a bloody miracle.
Could it really be Kjeragandr's protection?
Isn't it the other way round? A thousand against one, and she's still breathing...
Maybe it really is Kjeragandr's protection.
It's not.
Kjeragandr is kind and watchful, but this result is not the result of Her power.
That no one lost their life here is a miracle that we created together.
That's what I believe.
Y-You're the Saintess of Kjerag?
Your words honor us more than we deserve.
What are your instructions?
I wouldn't call it instructions, per se.
Just an invitation to attend the post-ceremony banquet, as is the place of the host.
After all, aren't you here in Kjerag for the ceremony?
...I thought exile would be the best-case scenario.
We're allowed to participate in the banquet?
Would you rather be here for the banquet, or admit that you're here for a 'military operation' and go to prison?
Uhm.
Go on, be my guest.
Saintess.
Everything is ready. The banquet can begin at any tie.
Thank you, Kjarr.
Why thank me?
No particular reason... just wanted to thank you.
Kjerag will become a better place, Kjarr.
There will come a day when people from the outside will know more than just Karlan Trade, see more than just the snow, mountains and valleys.
They will know that there is a country here by the name of Kjerag.
A respectable country with its unique culture and customs, where its people live in prosperity.
Sounds great.
You sure you don't need 'a little help', Enya?
Without that, you wouldn't have any rainbows.
Kjeragandr's blessing reaches into the heart because of how precious it is.
If we ask Kjeragandr for a miracle for every little thing, then what's left is not religion, but reliance.
When you start to take something for granted, that's when you stop respecting it.
Things are fine the way it is.
Let's just hope Kjeragandr doesn't get angry at me for thinking this way.
Hah, don't worry, Enya.
Nothing makes a mother happier than to see her child start to walk on its own two feet.
I'm done, Mr. Silverash.
This is my first outdoor speech. Are you sure this is going to work?
It was perfect, Mr. Mob.
Your voice was filled with passion. No one could pick a flaw in it.
Even though you declined my offer to have someone magnify your voice with Originium Arts, the amplification device that you used performed above and beyond expectations.
Is this your company's new product?
This amplifier?
No, of course not. It's good, but the market for amplifiers is cornered by a few big companies. There's no place for us there... ahem. Forget I said anything.
I've heard about corporate competition in Kazimierz. You didn't say anything that I didn't know.
So, what is it that you're using?
A gift from a friend I met on the train.
They probably thought it was just a toy.
Who could have thought that it would come in so handy when Mr. Silverash made that abrupt request?
I see...
Mr. Mob, Karlan Trade would very much love for our cooperation to extend beyond this one time.
I will have someone take you around Kjerag for the next few days, including tours of our mines and factories.
Please accept this as a token of my sincerity.
I believe Kjerag will not disappoint you.
They stopped. They really stopped.
It's like you said. That man in a suit opens his mouth, and both sides stop fighting before long!
I thought we were in for a real fight. Didn't SilverAsh tell you guys to get here quick?
But it was all over in an instant.
Why did the Victorians and Kjerags stop fighting as soon as he arrived?

opts: Because he's a spokesman for the Kazimierz General Chamber of Commerce.

lines.x: Who...? Is that really important?
Oh, wait, I know!
The important thing is that he's from Kazimierz, isn't it?

Victoria wants to bully Kjerag for its own interests, but they have to be careful when it comes to Kazimierz, who's also here for its own interests... I think something like this came up in one of Anna's tests.
So... is this why SilverAsh contacted you and Muelsyse?
You're his backup plan?

opts: You're learning, Leto.
We don't need it now, though.

lines.x: No wonder you were in such a hurry.
Stuff like this is a pain.
But how did you know that the man in the suit was a spokesman for... hm, for what again?
You know him?

opts: We've met.
His appearance has changed, but his voice hasn't.

lines.x: Oh yeah, you and Amiya went to Kazimierz for a while.
I want to go with Mamochka, if I get the chance.
There are a few knights I really like. They're so cool!
Appearance... voice?
I guess his voice was pretty unusual. It was really loud.
I don't know how he did it. I could hear him all the way from here.
Oh yeah, Doctor.
Did you give him something at the station?

opts: A little toy that Closure made.
Just a little help.

lines.x: Really?
Somehow, I can't help but think there's more than meets the eye.
Oh... Muelsyse is chatting with someone.
Should we go over?
You have people you know here too, don't you, Sharp?
I'm working right now.

opts: The one who invited me is probably a little too busy right now.
Taking in the view here isn't such a bad idea.

lines.x: Sure.
I'll stay with you.

opts: I don't think that's going to work.
There's someone waiting for you there.

lines.x: For me? Who? Oh...
HIM.

opts: Are you not going?

lines.x: Well, there really isn't anything I want to say to him..
Arrggghhh! This sucks!
Sorry, Doctor, Sharp, give me a minute!
I'll be back soon!
Rosalind.
You got something to say, spit it out.
Otherwise, I've got places to be.
Wait!
I just wanted to make sure that you're safe.
I couldn't come find you in that chaos.
I'm fine.
The Victorians weren't looking for me. Even that Trilby Asher guy is no match for Sharp and the Doctor.
That bastard's still after you?!
I don't think so. It didn't look like he was here for me.
Well, he's lucky he ran away. Sharp and I would have given him a nice little beating.
Don't underestimate a Trilby Asher.
I don't like their methods either, but the Trilby Ashers are a big deal.
Him? Didn't seem like he was anything special.
That's because Enciodes has Degenbrecher to keep him down!
A warrior must never underestimate an enemy, Rosalind.
Alright, you done then? I gotta go.
Wait!
Three.
Two.
R-Rosalind!
...I spent a lot of time thinking about it last night.
I was weak back then.
The days with your mother were like honey. I couldn't keep myself from thinking about her. I couldn't keep myself away from her, from the very beginning.
I dragged you and Tatyana into that mess, but abandoned you when I couldn't handle the pressure...
You were right to yell at me, Rosalind.
Whatever I say now won't be anything more than an excuse.
For more than a decade, my memory has tried to justify it.
It whitewashes the memories for a man too weak to face his own weaknesses, so that he can continue to feel safe, continue to run away.
He lied so much he even fooled himself.
It's my fault.
I deserve to be scolded and scorned by you.
And I can't blame you for being disappointed in me and Kjerag...
I let you and your mother down.
I'm sorry.
There's one thing I want to make clear.
Mamochka never said anything bad about Kjerag.
Or about my papa.
She even taught me how to pray to Kjeragandr, even though I never really believed in it.
Mamochka only ever said good things about this place.
Tatyana... she doesn't hate Kjerag?
Why would I lie?
I told you, didn't I? She said my papa was really handsome.
Of course, I'm having doubts about that now.
It was before I started in with the beard. Tatyana did say she liked my face...
Wait, why did she tell you all this... could it be...?
Could it be that she doesn't blame me?
Stop.
Don't get too ahead of yourself.
Don't count on other people not blaming you, if you know you're wrong.
But it's exhausting to live with hatred. You underestimate Mamochka, if you thought she would spend a decade hating you and Kjerag.
You're not worth it.
You're right...
It's not worth it to live in pain for a man like me.
It's all for the better...
Good, you get it.
I didn't know who my papa was, and now I do. But that's all there is to it.
I don't need recognition from your family. Paleroche business is none of my business.
I'm the daughter of Tatyana Yevgenyevna Larina.
I'm Rosalind Tatyanovna Larina, codename Leto. Remember that.
It was Tatyana who named you. Rosalind Tatyanovna Larina... rolls more smoothly off the tongue than Paleroche.
Leto... not bad, not bad. A nice codename.
At least you have taste.
Oh yeah.
This is for you.
A... bank account?
Child support for all these years. The amount is in the back. I rounded it down for you.
Please make the transfer at your earliest convenience.
Ch-Child support?
I said it yesterday, didn't I?
Living expenses, tuition, medical bills, emotional damages for a deadbeat dad... it's all there.
Gotta go.
W-Wait, Rosalind!
The... the currency conversion might take some time. I-I'll find someone to take care of it as soon as possible!
Rosalind!
Please, can I see Tatyana one more time? Just once...
In your dreams!
Is this your will too, Kjeragandr?
The matters of the world are beyond a mere mortal's ability to predict...
Master?
Why are you standing all the way up front? There's nothing to see in particular...
Just passing by.
You said you just became a grandfather the last time you came to pray, Lucas?
Yes!
My son's daughter. Good thing she looks like her mother.
That foreign woman my son insisted on marrying. From what's-the-name... Siracusa! Fierce woman, that.
Well, at least the kid of mine got a taker.
Were you against the marriage?
What's the point, if the young ones are in love?
I might have said a word or two in my younger days.
But now? Just let them be.
You said you wanted a warding stone for your granddaughter, didn't you?
Hoping that some of Kjeragandr and the Saintess's blessings would rub off, you know.
You can have this.
Th-This mark... it's a stone blessed by the Saintess herself?
Th-That's too precious for someone like me, Master!
Take it. Consider it a welcome gift for your granddaughter as she comes into this world.
I'll pray to Kjeragandr that the children of Kjerag can grow up without illness, pain, or sorrow.
By Kjeragandr.
By Kjeragandr.
Phew...
The Black Knight is definitely more than just a name. To think she wasn't even trying to kill...
A little too much for old bones like mine.
Sir, your injuries...
Just a scratch.
Don't want to miss the Saintess's banquet, after all.
It's not every day that I get to be called 'sir' by you, though.
I think I just lost my appetite, for some reason.
My condolences concerning your appetite, My Lord.
I will report the facts of your performance in Kjerag and the changes in the situation to the Duke.
Suit yourself.
I'm confident that I did nothing to displease Her Grace.
What about you, Trilby Asher?
The waters of Londinium you could muddle, but Kjerag gives you little room to maneuver.
No need to worry about me, My Lord.
I have complete the mission assigned to me by Her Grace.
Oh?
Kjerag is not worth devoting too much time and effort to, but there are elements that require the Duke's attention.
You mean...
We thought it was Karlan Trade, or Kjerag's geographical advantages as a natural fortress and commerce hub...
Seems like Her Grace is truly grounded... or visionary?
Are you here to visit the true form of Kjeragandr?
Of course.
Her Grace wants to know whether Kjeragandr truly exists in Kjerag.
In times of crisis, will Kjeragandr really help Her people?
Interesting.
And your conclusion?
I do not recommend that the Duke confront Kjerag militarily at this time.
Seeing the Kazimierzian corporations that publicly entered into partnership with Karlan Trade, I also ran into a Columbian scientist on the way here...
Force is not the smart plan.
Winning is easy, making money is hard. We're not Ursine. It's not worth it.
It's not every day that we agree with each other.
Did you hit your head or something?
Like you, I'm a pacifist.
Eww...
Just talk like you normally do. Please.
If it saves some time and effort.
My Lord.
Yes?
Are you familiar with this little trinket?
A burdenbeast blind box, they're all the rage right now.
Why would you buy this?
Well, that's not important. Open it up and let's see what's inside.
I've got a full set, but I'm missing the limited-edition secret version.
No one knows what the box holds until it's opened.
The mystery is what makes it alluring. Perhaps I shouldn't open it.
Well, are you going to open it or not?
I'll have it, if you're not going to open it.
I'll open it.
Hm? What's this?

What did you find? Let me see... mother of mercy!
This is...
Frozen crystal burdenbeast! Secret limited edition!
What kind of luck is that? I've been opening these boxes since the first day I arrived in Kjerag, and I've never gotten this one!
By Kjeragandr, this is not fair!
Trilby Asher, we're both in Her Grace's employ. Do you think you could...?
I'm afraid not, My Lord.
I must stay in Kjerag for a while longer after submitting my report.
Thanks to you, I just realized that... collecting burdenbeast accessories isn't such a bad pastime after all.
Can you stand?
Not dead yet. I'll be fine in a few days.
Good.
Gnosis?
He went down first.
He says he's footing the bill tonight.
He has lousy taste. Pick for him.
Okay.
You've been walking around in circles there for ages. You got something to say, out with it.
I'm thinking that my debt to you seems to keep growing, from Kazimierz to here.
One that I don't see a way to repay.
You're afraid I'd jump ship?
It would be a big loss.
You want to repay me?
Name your price. Gnosis will find a way.
Forget it.
All I did was follow an arrogant young man to these bleak frozen hills ten years ago on a whim, a place where the temperature hovers around freezing on the warmest days.
Life here didn't turn out to be so bad, so I started doing what I could.
What does any of that have to do with you, Enciodes?
I guess you're right.
You stayed for the land, not for me.
I didn't think I would hear it from your own mouth.
And don't put on that act about owing me.
Tell the truth. What are you thinking?
I'm thinking...
There will come a day when people from the outside will know more than just Karlan Trade, see more than just the snow, mountains and valleys.
They'll remember the name 'Kjerag'.
Don't get too excited.
You may have won this round, but at the end of the day you've just gotten yourself even more tough customers.
Should I thank you for bringing Kazimierz back to me?
Enciodes stays silent for a moment, then picks up a broken piece of ice from the ground and points it at the sun.
The ice is dauntingly cold, but it glitters under the sun, almost glaringly.
Yes. Kazimierz has come, Columbia is coming.
Things will only get more complicated.
You can't win every time.
Have you ever lost?
No.
That's too bad. You must never have met an opponent like me.
Oh?
Come with Gnosis to the Tschäggättä's training grounds. I'll take both of you on at the same time.
One hand tied behind my back.
Haha. Gnosis is actually not bad with a sword.
You're in a good mood.
It's all done.
Three years of perseverance have borne fruit at last.
We broke things wide open. Caster is no longer an existential threat.
The independence of Kjerag is no longer a pipe dream.
In order words, you've drawn the attention of both arrogant Victorian nobles and unscrupulous Kazimierz merchants.
Soon to be joined by scientists with no qualms about moral or ethics.
Even more people will hate you for bringing even more chaos to this country.
Let them.
I don't care.
All I care is—
Excuse me—
Are you the Black Knight?
...Yes.
So it's true! The Black Knight is in Kjerag!
It's really the Black Knight! This makes it all worth it!
And you... you're the president of Karlan Trade, aren't you?
I've been a fan of the Black Knight since I was five. Can I get her autograph?
That's up to her.
Do you want to sign for these young folks, Degenbrecher?
...Fine.
The campaign knights eagerly call out to their comrades.
A little line forms on the ice.
It is like one of the many small fan meet-and-greets on the streets of Kazimierz.
The campaign knights give Degenbrecher their cards, and Degenbrecher writes her name on them.
Enciodes chats with the knights every now and then, playing the part of a good host.
It is all very natural. No one has the slightest complaint.
Eventually, the knights leave, satisfied, and calm returns to the ice.
The campaign knights I know aren't that excitable.
Our partnership with Kazimierz has just begun. The Adeptus Sprawiedliwi Kazimierz wouldn't have sent 'real' campaign knights.
This is just the Browntails spending their own money to invite some youths over for a sightseeing trip.
And it probably took the Black Knight's name to get them to agree.
Heh.
How do you feel?
Maybe I'm no different from the Kazimierz that you hated. You're just another chip in my hands.
All of it is the same, be it your identity as the Black Knight, Kazimierz's exploitation of you, your martial strength, and your value as a soldier.
Do you find this unacceptable?
Now?
I've gotten used to it.
Let us once again welcome our friends from far away.
The doors of Kjerag are always open to those who come in good faith. Let us remember the friendships forged today.
By Kjeragandr.
By Kjeragandr.
By Kjeragandr!
By Kjeragandr!
Thank you for the invitation, Saintess.
You're... Muelsyse, from Rhine Lab.
It warms me up that you still remember me.
The mountains and rivers of Kjerag that I saw along the way were awfully pretty. I'm sure we'll have a great partnership.
Rhine Lab's partner is Karlan Trade, Miss Muelsyse.
But you are the one calling the shots in Kjerag.
Building a launch pad on Karlan is a big deal. Mr. Silverash has stressed the need to get the Saintess's blessing.
I have heard about this from Sir Enciodes.
Karlan is the spine and body of Kjeragandr. I must say I have reservations about such a massive project taking place there—
Saintess, Saintess.
Excuse me.
What is it, Kjarr?
That launch pad that they're talking about, it sends things into the sky, right?

If they build it there...
Does it mean that I...?
Er, Kjeragandr, will get a few cannons on Her body?
Cannons...
Kjarr... do you think Kjeragandr would object?
Object? Why?
It sounds so cool!
You should say yes!
Lisburn! What happened?
Those wounds...
It's nothing.
Freya, I have something important to tell you...
What is it, Lisburn?
I... love...
'I ♥ Snow'! The knitted words on your clothes look so nice!
The snowy mountains are so beautiful on a fine day like this!
Huh? Oh, uh, yeah.
Aw damn it all!
The berk ran away from THAT? I should shoot him for desertion!
Keep it down, Jack!
Did you all peep on me like this, back in the day, when I proposed to my wife?
Come now, Harold, this isn't personal. We do this to everyone who proposes.
Is Lisburn really going to propose after a fight like that?
What does he have to lose after a fight like that?
She says yes, he gets to bury his bones here. She says no, he gets closure and returns to Victoria. It's going to take him the better part of a full year to recover.
You have a point.
You idiots, why didn't you call me over to help?
Are you going to let your brother propose empty-handed? No ring, fine, but not even a flower?
It's all ice and snow here. Where are you gonna find a flower?
Make an ice flower, then! What do you carry your weapons for? Nothing shows sincerity like the handmade stuff. That's what the ladies like, don't you understand?
Well, you're in a better position to understand than any of us...
Right, Harold? Ice flower, eh? I'm sure there's someone back home who will be very interested to know.
W-Wait! Don't you run your mouth in front of my wife!
I've never given an ice flower to any woman other than Lily... it was just an example! Really!
I didn't expect to see you here.
It's my job to protect the Doctor.
Same old story.
Care to spar?
After you've fully recovered.
We can do it after hours, if I'm still in Kjerag then.
Alright.
How many people do you have who are better than you?
I wouldn't mind making the trip myself, if there are enough of them.
You used to commentate the knight competitions?
No wonder your voice sounded familiar. Maybe I watched a match you were covering!
Thanks for the compliment. Not that I have much to write home about other than my voice!
I didn't expect to see the campaign knights here, though...
The ones in silver armor? They're so cool!
I invited them here!
And you, too, Rosalind! You're a guest of mine as well.
Enjoy the hospitality of Kjerag!
I must thank you, Doctor.
Thank you for accepting the invitation, and for your little gift to Mr. Mob.
It was quite helpful.

opts: You had a plan even without my help, didn't you?
Save it, I know you always have a backup plan.

lines.x: It would have involved more effort.
Under the circumstances, I'm plenty thankful just to speed things up by a step.
I hope you're happy with our hospitality.

opts: Don't think you can hook me with an invitation letter next time.
Was it you who invited Rhine? I thought Rhodes Island was the extent of your manipulation.

lines.x: Looks like you still remember our game of chess three years ago, Doctor.
Our relationship has changed over the past three years. There's no need for this level of caution.
Karlan Trade invited Rhine Lab to discuss cooperation.
But I, Enciodes, invited you, Doctor, for the sole purpose of attending the ceremony, and nothing else.
You can take part of the credit for this festive scene before us.
Come, ally.
I will be happy to be your guide, if you would like to see what Kjerag is like now.

opts: I guess we could do worse than a shore leave.
I'll be your guest, then, so to speak.

lines.x: One more thing.

opts: Next time, if you want me to bring someone with me, just say it.
Are you surprised that she didn't come?

lines.x: As an ally, I'm sure you understand what I mean.

opts: Cliffheart is doing fine. It's just that this was on short notice, and she happened to be away from Rhodes Island.
Your sister is busy with her own business. Send the invitation earlier next time.
She'll come back to Kjerag next time.
She said she'll give you a surprise. The plural 'you'.

lines.x: Glad to hear that.
It's a shame that she couldn't come on such an important occasion, but if she continues to climb, then whatever peak she reaches...
She'll hear the wind of Karlan from the top.
...Ratatos?
You shouldn't be here now. The absence of the head of one of the three great houses at the Saintess's banquet will draw unnecessary attention.
Sciurus will take care of things.
If there's anyone who shouldn't be here at this time, it's the CTO of Karlan Trade.
I'm sure that director of Rhine Lab has many things she wants to discuss with someone in a technical position.
Enciodes will take care of her needs.
We want Rhine Lab's technology, and they want Karlan's elevated terrain. Fundamentally, this is a business deal.
That's more in Enciodes's domain.
I guess you're right.
I'm thinking that Enciodes must have had a dozen contingency plans for today's incident.
He put up a good act of being worried, though. Did it fool you?
That's the kind of person he is. I'm not surprised.
But there's one thing that you're off the mark about, Ratatos.
This is the rare occasion that he didn't put up an act.
Yes... It's not like him. Not sufficiently profit-driven, too much risk, yet the best outcome was achieved for everyone.
*Sigh*.
You hear that, Gnosis? It's the sound of metal coming together to form a monster.
I've listened to the heavy noise of construction for long enough.
Project One is near completion, from what I've heard.
You heard right.
But for all the effort we committed, even building Kjeragandr's statue as a ruse and building the base underneath Lake Silberneherze...
For all the time we put in, the effort it took to hide it all—
One battleship is all we managed to build.
We can't hide it for long. Even if Victoria lets it go this once, the world will eventually figure out what we're doing.
That's why we don't need to hide it on purpose.
Just try not to draw too much attention.
I'm sure you realize why Victoria stopped.
We must develop as much as we can now, while all the powers at work are still in balance.
This is the only way out for Kjerag.
I hope it all goes as well as you say.
Speaking of which, Ratatos.
Yes?
I heard some rumors.
Are you really going to call the Project One ship the 'Walnut'?
Heh.
Why not?
That silly sister of mine came up with an interesting idea this time.
She said that when she has her own kids, she'll tell them that this ship is their older brother.
Cute, isn't it?
Rosalind?
I'm sorry I couldn't come with you to Kjerag this time.
You took the box to the top of the mountain, didn't you?
Mamochka!
You can trust me.
You went out again? The doctor told you to stay in bed!
Just taking a walk. I'm fine.
All this fuss over one broken bone. I'm getting suffocated, stuck in a hospital room all day.
That's because you weren't careful when fiddling with that jetpack...
It was fun, wasn't it?
I want to try it again sometime. Did you have the chance to try out rock-climbing while you were in Kjerag?
No, I didn't.
You really should, next time you have a chance. It's fun.
I'll take you on an adventure in Sargon once my leg's healed... Sami sounds good too!
Hehe.
I can't wait!
